data.title: Spotify Stock (SPOT) Bulls Rejoice as Operating Leverage Becomes Key Growth Catalyst
data.description:
Spotify (SPOT) has steadily transformed from a fast-growing streaming service into a high-margin, mission-critical audio utility — one that now feels as indispensable as a monthly water or power bill. Last week's Q4 earnings reinforced a pivotal shift: the business is no longer just scaling users, but scaling profitability. Operating leverage is finally showing up in a meaningful way as content costs normalize, monetization improves, and fixed infrastructure is spread across a massive global listener base.

data.title: Options Market Flags Bullish Signal on Super Micro Computer Stock (SMCI)
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has really been one of the more disappointing names within the artificial-intelligence ecosystem, although this underperformance could probably be best explained by where the company sits in the AI supply chain. Essentially, from a physical product standpoint, SMCI stock represents a downstream investment, which is a difficult place to be in tech.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Essentially, at the top of the pyramid stand semiconductor foundries, which effectively get paid based on order flow, which is already intense. Foundries are one of the top beneficiaries of the AI narrative because they essentially sell tickets to the game; thus, it doesn't matter who ultimately wins the matchup, which is more of a concern for chip designers. It's a bit like selling shovels in a gold rush.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Why Investors Should Avoid Catching the Pinterest (PINS) Falling Knife
data.description:
Pinterest (PINS) stock has been a mess lately. The stock sold off sharply after reporting mixed Q4 results last week and issuing guidance that the market viewed as weak, even though key operating metrics continued growing. In 2026 alone, PINS shares have already fallen more than 40%, reflecting a clear misalignment between execution, projections, and consensus expectations—driven by sector headwinds, management credibility concerns, and ongoing AI-related threats.

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Launches Video Podcasts
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is further diversifying its business with the launch of video podcasts.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starting this spring, the iPhone maker will bring new integrated video podcasts to its Apple Podcast platform. The move comes as video viewership continues to reshape podcasting. About 37% of people over age 12 watch video podcasts monthly, according to data from Edison Research.

The new video podcasts put Apple up against established players in the podcast space such as Spotify (SPOT), Alphabet-owned (GOOGL) YouTube, and Netflix (NFLX), all of whom are expanding their video podcasting content.

Apple's History with Podcasts

In a news release, Apple sought to remind people of its history with podcasts. "Twenty years ago, Apple helped take podcasting mainstream by adding podcasts to iTunes, and more than a decade ago, we introduced the dedicated Apple Podcasts app," said the consumer electronics giant in its statement.

Within the Apple Podcasts app, users will be able to switch between watching and listening to shows from the same feed. They can also download video podcast episodes for offline viewing and live stream certain video podcasts from the app.

YouTube said recently that it has more than one billion monthly active viewers of video podcast content on its platform.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 27 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $306.89 implies 12.26% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: SpaceX Enters Pentagon's Contest to Build Voice-Controlled Military Drone Swarms
data.description:
Elon Musk's aerospace company, SpaceX, along with its subsidiary xAI, is quietly competing in a new Pentagon contest that's focused on developing voice-controlled, autonomous drone-swarming technology. The program, which offers a $100 million prize, wants to develop systems that can translate spoken commands into digital instructions and coordinate multiple drones at once. The six-month competition is being run jointly by the Defense Innovation Unit and the Defense Autonomous Warfare Group, which is a new branch within the U.S. Special Operations Command.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The goal is to create drone swarms that can operate in both the air and the sea, while also being able to share target information and potentially carry out missions from launch to finish. While flying multiple drones simultaneously is already possible, building software that allows them to move autonomously as a coordinated swarm remains a major technical challenge. At the same time, the project raises ethical and operational concerns.

For example, generative AI tools like large language models are known to produce biased or inaccurate outputs, which has made some defense officials worried about giving them too much authority in combat settings. In addition, although SpaceX is already a major defense contractor, its entry into offensive AI-enabled weapons development is a notable change for Musk. Indeed, he has previously warned against designing weapons that can kill without meaningful human control.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: UBS Upgrades Rivian Stock (RIVN) After 27% Surge – But Sees Limited Upside
data.description:
Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) investors haven't had much to cheer about in recent years, but it's safe to say shareholders headed off to the long weekend in a positive frame of mind. Shares of the EV maker surged by ~27% in Friday's session after the company beat expectations in its Q4 readout. More importantly, it signaled that its upcoming mass market model will lead to a jump in deliveries.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rivian intends to launch its new R2 model in Q2, priced at just under $45,000, which is comparable to Tesla's Model Y. This represents a significant discount compared to the premium R1 lineup. For the still unprofitable company, the R2 SUVs should boost 2026 deliveries by 53%, reaching between 62,000 and 67,000 units, at the midpoint, topping consensus (Visible Alpha) at 64,130 vehicles.

The healthy outlook has caused UBS analyst Joseph Spak to rethink his RIVN model.

"We believe the near-term risk/reward is more balanced at current levels as our prior rating was in part valuation based," Spak said.

Ultimately, the analyst added, the RIVN investment thesis centers on the company evolving into a larger and, eventually, profitable business. Spak says he has consistently been enthusiastic about its products, upcoming lineup, and brand strength. The concern, however, has been that the valuation had "gotten ahead of itself."

Rivian's 2026 delivery guidance of 62,000–67,000 units is better than Spak expected, and if the company can achieve it, it suggests a strong year-end run rate that could set the stage for even stronger performance in 2027 and beyond.

But looking into the details, Spak believes the guide assumes R1 and RCV volumes remain flat year-over-year. However, he anticipates R1 demand could decline due to the loss of EV tax credits and other regulatory rollbacks affecting EVs. Additionally, with Rivian set to release more advanced ADAS hardware in the second half of the year, there's a "reasonable probability" some early adopters might decide to delay their purchases.

Essentially, Spak sees limited room for upside to the 2026 guide, while downside remains a possibility. This is because the guidance assumes roughly 45,000 units in the second half – a "strong ramp" – and about 22,000 R2 deliveries, which, while demand seems solid, would require a "near flawless production execution." As a result, Spak expects minimal positive revisions in the near term. Additionally, the company continues to burn cash, with positive EBITDA unlikely for several years.

"We'd expect the stock to remain volatile, particularly driven by sentiment swings," the analyst summed up.

While Spak has now upgraded his RIVN rating from Sell to Neutral and his price target goes from $15 to $16, the post-earnings surge has taken the shares 10% above the new target. (To watch Spak's track record, click here)

A Hold is also the consensus view here, a rating based on 8 Buys and Holds, each, plus 4 Sells. Going by the $17.7 average target, the shares will stay rangebound for the time being. (See RIVN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Capex Spending Raises the Stakes as META's Core Business Shines
data.description:
I've long been impressed by the way Meta Platforms (META) has navigated difficult environments, repeatedly reinventing itself through thick and thin while steadily building its cash position. Even amid mounting geopolitical uncertainty, management continues to deploy capital aggressively into ambitious AI initiatives, signaling confidence in the company's long-term competitive positioning. At the same time, capital expenditures remain elevated as Meta invests heavily in data centers and infrastructure to support its AI roadmap.

data.title: Tesla Adds Grok to Its Vehicles in Nine European Countries
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) is bringing xAI's Grok to its vehicles across Europe, starting with nine countries. According to Tesla's Europe, Middle East & Africa account on X, the first phase includes the United Kingdom, Ireland, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, Italy, France, Portugal, and Spain, with more markets expected to be added later. Interestingly, Grok will allow drivers to ask questions using real-time information and interact with the system without taking their hands off the wheel.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, the feature includes selectable personalities, such as "Storyteller" and "Unhinged," that will give drivers different response styles. To activate Grok, users can either open it through the App Launcher or press and hold the microphone button on the steering wheel. However, Grok is only available on Model S, Model 3, Model X, Model Y, and Cybertruck vehicles that have an AMD (AMD) infotainment processor.

Vehicles must also be running software version 2025.26 or newer, and navigation command support requires version 2025.44.25 or later. Moreover, Tesla noted that Grok does not replace standard voice commands for vehicle controls like climate settings or media changes. The company also said that Grok interactions are processed securely by xAI and are not connected to individual drivers or specific vehicles. For now, users do not need a separate Grok account or a subscription to access the feature.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat after Another Soft Week Driven by AI Concerns
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were little changed Monday evening. The market is coming off another down week as worries about AI-driven disruption weighed on real estate, trucking, and financial services. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.1% and 0.09%, respectively, at 6:17 p.m. EDT on February 16, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) futures were down 0.19%

The New York Stock Exchange was closed earlier in the day in observance of Presidents' Day.

In the last week, both the S&P 500 and the Dow fell more than 1%. The Nasdaq dropped over 2%, marking its fifth straight weekly decline and its longest losing streak since 2022.

Looking ahead, the Home Builder Confidence Index and Empire State manufacturing survey are scheduled for release tomorrow. On the earnings front, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) will release its quarterly numbers on Tuesday after the bell.

data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Wins Over Analysts — But Is the Upside Already Priced In?
data.description:
Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) stock might not be one of the market's fashionable names, but that hasn't stopped it from outperforming the S&P 500 by a wide margin in recent years. Shares have climbed 15% year-to-date, compared with a flat showing for the S&P 500. That follows gains of 72% in 2024 and 23% in 2025, well ahead of the index's respective returns of 23% and 16%.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With the retail giant set to report Q4 earnings on Thursday (Feb 19), Oppenheimer analyst Rupesh Parikh thinks the company is about to deliver a performance that will feature "growth across all categories" in the US business.

Based on his analysis, Parikh has raised his Q4 EPS estimate toward the upper end of management's guidance range, reflecting expectations for a robust holiday season, continued top-line momentum across the business, and potentially modest weather-related benefits.

The analyst now forecasts Q4 EPS of $0.72, up from $0.70, compared with the guide of $0.67–0.72 and the Street's estimate of $0.72.

That said, Parikh expects the company's FY26 (Jan. 2027) guide to land below current Street estimates, similar to the approach taken last year and more consistent with WMT's longer-term algorithm. Recall, the company initially guided to EPS of $2.50–2.60 last year vs. the Street's forecast of $2.77. Consensus estimates for FY26 call for 4.9% sales growth and more than 11% operating income growth.

All told, Parikh remains a big fan of the company. "We still believe a premium valuation is justified given our expectations for continued strong levels of sales and profit growth, ongoing market share gains, a still long runway in scaling alternative revenue businesses, and optionality related to the company's Indian assets," the 5-star analyst summed up.

Accordingly, Parikh rates WMT shares an Outperform (i.e., Buy), and to "reflect the recent re-rating in the space," lifted his price target from $125 to $140, suggesting the stock will gain 4.5% over the coming months. (To watch Parikh's track record, click here)

RBC analyst Steven Shemesh also calls for adj. EPS of $0.72 and says his pre-quarter work "points to continued momentum." Shemesh believes the company is strongly positioned to gain market share and improve its margin profile over time, driven by above-average growth in alternative revenue streams such as advertising.

That said, given the stock's outperformance since reporting Q3 earnings, and the fact that heading into the print, expectations seem high, the analyst thinks the risk/reward tilts negative.

Nevertheless, while Shemesh expects the multiple to "remain debated," he too rates the shares as Outperform (Buy) and his price target also goes to $140 (up from $126). (To watch Shemesh's track record, click here)

Almost all other Street analysts are thinking along the same lines; based on 26 Buys vs. 1 lone Hold, the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, the $133.04 average price target suggests shares are fully valued right now. With this in mind, keep an eye out for either other price target hikes or rating downgrades shortly. (See WMT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
